info.title: GenStudio API
info.description:
API for Adobe GenStudio for Performance Marketing users to retrieve approved GenStudio experiences.

**Experimental API**: These endpoints are experimental and require the `X-Adobe-Accept-Experimental: 1` header on every request. Requests without this header will receive a `400 Bad Request` response. This header signals that the consumer acknowledges the experimental nature of these APIs and that breaking changes may occur without prior notice. Note: this header requirement is temporary and will be removed once the API graduates to stable.

assetId.description: Raw composite asset identifier. Uniquely identifies the asset across GenStudio organizations. For assets that originate in a different GenStudio organization than the experience, use this value to look up or retrieve the asset directly.

url.description: Asset URL. For same-organization assets: proxied through this API — token-backed, always resolves to a presigned download URL. For cross-organization assets (typical for email and display channel experiences): a best-effort direct delivery URL with no embedded token. Works for publicly accessible assets; may return 401/403 for private ones — use assetId to retrieve those assets independently. Absent when the asset reference is a bare URN with no delivery host information.

url.description: Asset URL. For same-organization assets: proxied through this API — token-backed, always resolves to a presigned download URL. Works for publicly accessible assets; may return 401/403 for private ones — use assetId to retrieve those assets independently. Absent when the asset reference is a bare URN with no delivery host information.

type.description: A URI reference [RFC3986](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986) to a URL, which, when dereferenced, provides human readable documentation for the problem type.  The problem type may be equivalent to the `status` or may be a "sub-type".  This URI is not intended to be parsed by clients; rather it should be treated as an opaque string that uniquely represents the error condition as-is.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type.

status.description: The HTTP status code ([RFC9110], Section 15) generated by the origin server for this occurrence of the problem.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

requestId.description: A unique identifier that is assigned to the request, either by the API caller or by the service itself.

get.description:
Retrieves a single experience by ID, including all fields, variants, and asset URLs.

Supports conditional requests via the `If-None-Match` header. If the provided ETag matches, returns `304 Not Modified` with no body.

**Backward compatibility**: New optional fields may be added to the response at any time. Clients must tolerate unknown properties.

**Rate limit**: 60 requests per minute per organization.

parameters.name: experienceId
parameters.description: GenStudio experience URN.

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token from Adobe OAuth Server-to-Server credentials

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Client ID from the Adobe Developer Console project

parameters.name: x-ims-org-id
parameters.description: IMS Organization ID (format: {HEX}@AdobeOrg). Found in Adobe Developer Console: open your project, then Credentials → OAuth Server-to-Server → Credential Details → Organization ID.

parameters.name: x-request-id
parameters.description: Client-generated UUID for request tracing

parameters.name: x-correlation-id
parameters.description: Correlation ID to group related requests

parameters.name: x-adobe-accept-experimental
parameters.description: Required opt-in header for experimental API endpoints. Must be set to "1". Requests without this header will receive a 400 Bad Request response.

headline.value: Bold colors, clean lines — designed for everyday momentum.

401.description: Unauthorized. Indicates that the request has not been applied because it lacks valid authentication credentials for the target resource.

403.description: Forbidden. Indicates that the server understood the request but refuses to fulfil it.

404.description: Not Found. Indicates that the origin server did not find a current representation for the target resource or is not willing to disclose that one exists.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Indicates that the target resource does not have a current representation that would be acceptable to the user agent, according to the proactive negotiation header fields received in the request.

429.description: Too Many Requests. Indicates that the user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time ("rate limiting").

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of Experience summaries filtered by query parameters.

Supports Cursor-based pagination via the `cursor` parameter. Use the `cursor` value from the previous response to retrieve the next page.

Supports conditional requests via the `If-None-Match` header. If the provided ETag matches, returns `304 Not Modified` with no body.

**Filters** (OR semantics within each): `languages`, `channel`, `createdAtFrom`, `createdAtTo`.

**Backward compatibility**: New optional fields may be added to items in the response at any time. Clients must tolerate unknown properties.

**Rate limit**: 20 requests per minute per organization.

parameters.name: languages
parameters.description: BCP 47 language tag to filter by, exact match (case-insensitive)

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: Filter by channel. Accepted values: email, meta, meta_organic, display, ctv, linkedin, linkedin_organic, tiktok, chatgpt, youtube. Unknown values return 400.

parameters.name: createdAtFrom
parameters.description: Inclusive lower bound (ISO 8601) on createdAt.

parameters.name: createdAtTo
parameters.description: Exclusive upper bound (ISO 8601) on createdAt. Must be strictly greater than createdAtFrom.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items per page (1–50).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from the previous response's `cursor` field.

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token from Adobe OAuth Server-to-Server credentials

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Client ID from the Adobe Developer Console project

parameters.name: x-ims-org-id
parameters.description: IMS Organization ID (format: {HEX}@AdobeOrg). Found in Adobe Developer Console: open your project, then Credentials → OAuth Server-to-Server → Credential Details → Organization ID.

parameters.name: x-request-id
parameters.description: Client-generated UUID for request tracing

parameters.name: x-correlation-id
parameters.description: Correlation ID to group related requests

parameters.name: x-adobe-accept-experimental
parameters.description: Required opt-in header for experimental API endpoints. Must be set to "1". Requests without this header will receive a 400 Bad Request response.

400.description: Bad Request. Indicates that the server cannot process the request due to something that is perceived to be a client error (e.g. invalid query parameters or createdAtFrom >= createdAtTo).

type.description: A URI reference [RFC3986](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986) to a URL, which, when dereferenced, provides human readable documentation for the problem type.  The problem type may be equivalent to the `status` or may be a "sub-type".  This URI is not intended to be parsed by clients; rather it should be treated as an opaque string that uniquely represents the error condition as-is.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type.

status.description: The HTTP status code ([RFC9110], Section 15) generated by the origin server for this occurrence of the problem.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

requestId.description: A unique identifier that is assigned to the request, either by the API caller or by the service itself.

schema.description: Bad Request. Indicates that the server cannot or will not process the request due to something that is perceived to be a client error. The Problem Details object will provide more information about the exact cause.

401.description: Unauthorized. Indicates that the request has not been applied because it lacks valid authentication credentials for the target resource.

403.description: Forbidden. Indicates that the server understood the request but refuses to fulfil it.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Indicates that the target resource does not have a current representation that would be acceptable to the user agent, according to the proactive negotiation header fields received in the request.

429.description: Too Many Requests. Indicates that the user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time ("rate limiting").

get.description:
Returns a **302 redirect** to a short-lived pre-signed URL for the requested asset. The 302 is the expected success response — there is no 200 body.

The client's HTTP library follows the redirect automatically. Each call issues a fresh redirect with a new token.

**Response headers on 302**: `Location` (signed URL, valid up to 60 minutes), `X-Asset-Mime-Type`, `Cache-Control: no-store`.

**Rate limit**: 60 requests per minute per organization.

parameters.name: experienceId
parameters.description: GenStudio experience URN.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: GenStudio asset URN

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token from Adobe OAuth Server-to-Server credentials

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Client ID from the Adobe Developer Console project

parameters.name: x-ims-org-id
parameters.description: IMS Organization ID (format: {HEX}@AdobeOrg). Found in Adobe Developer Console: open your project, then Credentials → OAuth Server-to-Server → Credential Details → Organization ID.

parameters.name: x-request-id
parameters.description: Client-generated UUID for request tracing

parameters.name: x-correlation-id
parameters.description: Correlation ID to group related requests

parameters.name: x-adobe-accept-experimental
parameters.description: Required opt-in header for experimental API endpoints. Must be set to "1". Requests without this header will receive a 400 Bad Request response.

302.description: Redirect to pre-signed asset delivery URL

Location.description: Pre-signed URL for the asset; valid for up to 60 minutes

X-Asset-Mime-Type.description: MIME type of the asset at the redirect destination

Cache-Control.description: Always no-store — prevents proxy caching so each call issues a fresh signed URL

401.description: Unauthorized. Indicates that the request has not been applied because it lacks valid authentication credentials for the target resource.

403.description: Forbidden. Indicates that the server understood the request but refuses to fulfil it.

404.description: Not Found. Indicates that the origin server did not find a current representation for the target resource or is not willing to disclose that one exists.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Indicates that the target resource does not have a current representation that would be acceptable to the user agent, according to the proactive negotiation header fields received in the request.

429.description: Too Many Requests. Indicates that the user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time ("rate limiting").

503.description: Service Unavailable. Indicates that the server is currently unable to handle the request due to a temporary overload or scheduled maintenance, which will likely be alleviated after some delay.
